title: The Owl and the Pussy-Cat
author: Edward Lear
source: Project Gutenberg eBook #13650 (Nonsense Songs, Stories, Botany, and Alphabets)
note: Three-stanza nonsense poem, one stanza per spread. Internal phrase repetition ('You are, You are, You are!') pulls TTR down despite Lear's invented vocabulary (Piggy-wig, bong-tree, runcible). Will exercise the prosody module when it lands — anapestic feet, ABABCC-leaning scheme.
syntaxNote: Syntax expectations hand-derived (M5): 10 sentences — verse accumulates across comma/colon line ends; '…You are!' splits before capitalized 'What'; Lear's own capitalized tag ('Your ring?" Said the Piggy') splits per the author's orthography. 18 clauses (semicolons in stanzas 1–3, 'where the bong-tree grows', 'and there… a Piggy-wig stood', relatives 'who'/'which'). Types 5 declarative / 2 interrogative / 3 exclamatory. Lengths spread 5–48 words → population stdev ≈ 14.5 (verse rhythm signal).
